Metadata.Tribunal_Court: High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd was incorporated in Singapore in 1998 as part of a group of related companies in the marine services industry known as the Mectrade Group. [cite: 21]
Before 14 October 2011, the shares in Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd were held by Foo Sack You and Foo Siew Wei in the ratio of 55:45 respectively. [cite: 21]

In or around early 2012, the shares in the Mectrade Group companies that had been held by Foo Sack You devolved to his widow, Chen Hong Hua, and their two children, Foo Su Mian and Foo Yong Yee. [cite: 21]
From early 2012, the shareholders of Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd were Chen Hong Hua, Foo Su Mian and Foo Yong Yee. [cite: 21]
Around 1975, Mectrade Engineering (Pte) Ltd was incorporated and became part of the Mectrade Group operated by Foo Sack You and Foo Tak Yi. [cite: 21]

Up to 14 October 2011, the Mectrade Group comprised several companies including Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd, Mectrade Fabricators Pte Ltd, Mectrade Engineering (Pte) Ltd, Sen-Hong Piping and Engineering Pte Ltd, Mec-Sin Precision Engineering Services, and Mec-Con Industries Pte Ltd. [cite: 21]

As at 14 October 2011, the shareholdings in the Mectrade Group companies were: Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd held 55% by Foo Sack You and 45% by Foo Siew Wei; Mectrade Fabricators Pte Ltd held 50% by Foo Sack You and 50% by Foo Tak Yi; Mectrade Engineering (Pte) Ltd held 50% by Foo Sack You and 50% by Foo Tak Yi; Sen-Hong Piping and Engineering Pte Ltd held 55% by Foo Sack You, 22.5% by Lee Hui, and 22.5% by the wife of Foo Tak Yi; Mec-Sin Precision Engineering Services was a sole proprietorship owned by Foo Sack You; Mec-Con Industries Pte Ltd was wholly owned by the family of the defendants. [cite: 21]

Between 2007 and 2014, Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd acted as a resident contractor of Dyna-Mac Engineering Services Pte Ltd at Dyna-Mac's shipyard at 45 Gul Road, Singapore 629350. [cite: 21]
From 2007 to 2014, Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd undertook mainly structural works such as steel fabrication for Dyna-Mac Engineering Services Pte Ltd as a resident contractor. [cite: 21]
During the period 2007 to 2014, Dyna-Mac Engineering Services Pte Ltd operated a shipyard with about 40 to 50 resident contractors, including Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd. [cite: 21]

The companies in the Mectrade Group, including Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd, shared common resources and engaged in inter-company transactions and inter-company billings over a period of about 30 years. [cite: 21]

Until 14 October 2011, the business of the Mectrade Group was operated by Foo Sack You and Foo Tak Yi, with assistance from family members including Foo Siew Wei and Foo Siew Ping in finance and accounting matters. [cite: 21]
Up to 14 October 2011, the registered directors of Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd were Foo Sack You and Foo Siew Wei. [cite: 21]
Before 14 October 2011, Foo Sack You mainly managed administrative and accounting matters for the Mectrade Group from the office, while Foo Tak Yi managed operations at shipyards. [cite: 21]
Before 14 October 2011, Foo Siew Wei and Foo Siew Ping worked in the office and assisted with finance and accounting matters for the Mectrade Group. [cite: 21]
Before 14 October 2011, Chen Hong Hua worked as personal assistant to her husband, Foo Sack You, and was rarely present in the office of the Mectrade Group companies. [cite: 21]
On 14 October 2011, Foo Sack You passed away. [cite: 21]

After the death of Foo Sack You and by early 2012, Foo Siew Ping was appointed as a director of Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd following consultation between her, Foo Siew Wei and Foo Tak Yi. [cite: 21]

On 12 September 2014, an extraordinary general meeting of Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd was held in the absence of Foo Siew Wei and Foo Siew Ping, at which two resolutions were passed to remove them as directors. [cite: 21]

On 1 December 2014, the Ministry of Manpower notified Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd that the work permits of its foreign workers had been revoked due to non-payment of foreign worker levy for September and October 2014. [cite: 21]

On 16 December 2014, Dyna-Mac Engineering Services Pte Ltd issued a cheque payment of $38,332.67 to Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd for foreign worker levy, as recorded in a Dyna-Mac payment voucher. [cite: 21]

On 17 December 2014, Dyna-Mac Engineering Services Pte Ltd issued a cheque payment of $12,740 to Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd for the cost of return air tickets for repatriating foreign workers, as recorded in a payment voucher. [cite: 21]

In 2014, Dyna-Mac Engineering Services Pte Ltd issued a cheque for $32,386.50 to Foo Siew Wei for the purpose of paying salaries of foreign workers engaged under Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd. [cite: 21]
Up to late 2014, Foo Tak Yi was involved in the day-to-day management and operations of Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd. [cite: 21]
Up to around 2014, all projects undertaken by Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd for Dyna-Mac Engineering Services Pte Ltd were supervised by Foo Tak Yi. [cite: 21]

Between 2005 and 2013, Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd received substantial revenue from Mectrade Engineering (Pte) Ltd and Mectrade Fabricators Pte Ltd for labour supply under inter-company arrangements. [cite: 21]
In October 2014, bank account statements of Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd recorded an entry labelled 'BFWL' for $13,500, which referred to foreign workers' levy. [cite: 21]
By 2014, the volume of work from Dyna-Mac Engineering Services Pte Ltd to Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd had decreased compared to earlier years. [cite: 21]

Around 2014, the work of Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd for Dyna-Mac Engineering Services Pte Ltd concluded, broadly coinciding with the removal of Foo Siew Wei and Foo Siew Ping as directors. [cite: 21]

From around early 2012, disputes arose between the shareholders of Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd and the defendants, leading to several lawsuits and culminating in the 2016 settlement agreement. [cite: 21]

Party_Details.Issue: Whether, based on pre-dispute conduct and inter-company dealings within the Mectrade Group, the directors and related individuals caused financial loss to Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd through conflict of interest, misappropriation of foreign worker funds, or the diversion of business opportunities.
Party_Details.Rule: Directors and operational managers must manage the company’s affairs in its best interests, ensuring proper documentation of funds and avoiding improper diversion of resources. Fiduciary obligations require that concurrent directorships do not disadvantage the company and that labour resources are managed consistently with established commercial practices and corporate policy.
Party_Details.Application: Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd operated as a resident contractor for Dyna-Mac, relying on a shared-resource structure with other Mectrade Group entities. Following the death of Foo Sack You, management remained with Foo Siew Wei and Foo Siew Ping, while Foo Tak Yi supervised on-site projects. The company received specific funds from Dyna-Mac for worker levies ($38,332.67), repatriation ($12,740), and salaries ($32,386.50). Allegations of misconduct were later raised concerning the redeployment of underutilised workers to group companies and the loss of business following the directors' removal in September 2014, which coincided with permit revocations by the Ministry of Manpower for non-payment of levies.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: Directorship of Foo Siew Wei and Foo Siew Ping within the Mectrade Group and the sharing of common resources do not inherently establish a conflict of interest, particularly where business activities are distinct. Alleged misappropriations of specific sums for foreign worker levies, salaries, and repatriation costs are subject to documentation proving the funds were applied for their intended purposes by Dyna-Mac. In the marine industry, maintaining a stable workforce despite uneven project flow is a commercially rational management practice. Consequently, the redeployment of underutilised workers to other group entities by Foo Tak Yi at established rates is consistent with prior corporate policy. Upon the removal of directors, fiduciary obligations cease, and subsequent business difficulties arising from the revocation of permits by the Ministry of Manpower due to non-payment of levies under new management do not constitute an actionable breach. Without a clearly defined cause of action against Foo Tak Yi and evidence of quantifiable loss, claims for breach of duty remain unsubstantiated.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Foo Siew Wei

Facts.Text: As at 14 October 2011, Foo Siew Wei held 45% of the shares in Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd, with the remaining 55% held by Foo Sack You. [cite: 21]
Up to 14 October 2011, Foo Siew Wei was a registered director of Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd together with Foo Sack You. [cite: 21]
Before 14 October 2011, Foo Siew Wei worked in the office of the Mectrade Group and assisted with finance and accounting matters. [cite: 21]

After the death of Foo Sack You and by early 2012, Foo Siew Wei remained a director of Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd and invited her sister, Foo Siew Ping, to assume the role of director after consulting her and their father, Foo Tak Yi. [cite: 21]

After early 2012, Foo Siew Wei did not consider it natural for members of Foo Sack You’s family to succeed him as director of Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd because they had not been working in the company. [cite: 21]

On 12 September 2014, an extraordinary general meeting of Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd was held in the absence of Foo Siew Wei, at which a resolution was passed to remove her as a director. [cite: 21]
Notice of the extraordinary general meeting on 12 September 2014 was stated to have been sent to Foo Siew Wei, but the notice document was not produced in this suit. [cite: 21]

In 2014, Dyna-Mac Engineering Services Pte Ltd issued a cheque for $32,386.50 to Foo Siew Wei for the purpose of paying salaries of foreign workers engaged under Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd. [cite: 21]

In late 2014, Foo Siew Wei liaised with Dyna-Mac Engineering Services Pte Ltd regarding payment of foreign workers' salaries and arrangements for repatriation after work permits were revoked. [cite: 21]

Party_Details.Issue: Whether management decisions regarding foreign worker salaries and participation in inter-company labour supply arrangements by Foo Siew Wei constituted a breach of fiduciary duty or misappropriation of funds.
Party_Details.Rule: A director must act in good faith and avoid conflicts of interest while managing corporate funds. Liability for misappropriation requires proof that funds were not applied for their intended business purpose. In a group context, redeploying labor consistent with established practice and commercial industry standards does not constitute an unauthorized diversion of assets.
Party_Details.Application: Foo Siew Wei served as a director and managed finance for the company until her removal in September 2014. She received a $32,386.50 salary cheque from Dyna-Mac to pay foreign workers and coordinated logistical responses following the revocation of permits by the Ministry of Manpower. The company maintained that these worker funds were misapplied and that labor was improperly diverted to other group entities managed by her family members.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: Holding concurrent roles in multiple entities within a corporate group does not create an inherent conflict of interest when the entities operate under distinct structural constraints and limited overlap. Claims of misappropriation regarding foreign worker funds are defeated by documentary evidence proving that the sums were paid for intended purposes such as levies, salaries, and repatriation, with no evidence of complaint from regulatory authorities. Commercial rationality dictates maintaining a stable workforce in a volatile industry, and the redeployment of workers at historical group rates constitutes a continuation of established business practice. Fiduciary duties terminate upon a director's removal, and liabilities cannot be imposed for subsequent business outcomes resulting from regulatory interventions or the actions of successor management.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Foo Siew Ping

Facts.Text: After the death of Foo Sack You and by early 2012, Foo Siew Ping was appointed as a director of Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd following an invitation from her sister, Foo Siew Wei, after consultation with their father, Foo Tak Yi. [cite: 21]
From early 2012 until her removal in 2014, Foo Siew Ping worked in the office of Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd and assisted with finance and accounting matters. [cite: 21]

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Foo Siew Ping's participation in the board and the handling of inter-company finance and labour arrangements resulted in an actionable breach of directorial duties.
Party_Details.Rule: Directors share a responsibility to participate in management in good faith and must exercise care in the handling of corporate resources. Overlapping directorships in a related company group are assessed for conflict based on the specific business activities and potential for actual prejudice to the entity.
Party_Details.Application: Foo Siew Ping was appointed to the board in 2012 and assisted with office accounting. She remained in office until her removal by resolution in late 2014. During this period, the company engaged in labor-sharing arrangements within the Mectrade Group and managed funds received from Dyna-Mac. The company alleged that she participated in a scheme to misappropriated funds and mismanaged workforce resources.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: Directorship in multiple related companies does not in itself establish a conflict of interest or a breach of duty. Liability for misappropriation or mismanagement of labour requires specific evidence linking the director to wrongful handling of corporate funds or resources. In a group environment with shared resources and established inter-company labour supply arrangements, the continuation of prior practices is commercially rational. Furthermore, a director's fiduciary obligations to the company end upon their formal removal from the board. Claims of loss must be particularised and supported by evidence demonstrating that specific business opportunities were diverted or that the director's conduct caused quantifiable damage to the entity.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Foo Tak Yi

Facts.Text: Around 1975, Mectrade Engineering (Pte) Ltd was incorporated and became part of the Mectrade Group operated by Foo Sack You and Foo Tak Yi, who entered into business together and grew their business as close friends. [cite: 21]
As at 14 October 2011, the shares in Mectrade Fabricators Pte Ltd and Mectrade Engineering (Pte) Ltd were held by Foo Sack You and Foo Tak Yi in the ratio of 50:50 respectively. [cite: 21]
As at 14 October 2011, the shares in Sen-Hong Piping and Engineering Pte Ltd were held 55% by Foo Sack You, 22.5% by Lee Hui, and 22.5% by the wife of Foo Tak Yi. [cite: 21]
The third defendant was in charge of operations and was on the ground at the relevant shipyards, while FSY mainly managed the backroom and handled administrative and accounting matters. [cite: 21]
Until late 2014, Foo Tak Yi was involved in the day-to-day management and operations of Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd. [cite: 21]
Between 2007 and 2014, all projects undertaken by Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd for Dyna-Mac Engineering Services Pte Ltd were supervised by Foo Tak Yi. [cite: 21]

From 2007 to 2014, Foo Tak Yi and the owner of Dyna-Mac Engineering Services Pte Ltd had a good personal and professional relationship, which facilitated the securing of several projects for Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd. [cite: 21]
Foo Tak Yi redeployed underutilised foreign workers sponsored for Hamid Marine Services & Engrg Pte Ltd to projects of other Mectrade Group companies. [cite: 21]

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Foo Tak Yi, in his operational capacity, breached duties regarding project supervision and the deployment of workforce resources to related group companies.
Party_Details.Rule: Operational management and project supervision are assessed for consistency with commercial rationality and established industry practice. The redeployment of underutilised workers among group companies does not establish an actionable wrong if conducted at appropriate rates and in accordance with prior corporate policy instituted by company controllers.
Party_Details.Application: Foo Tak Yi supervised the on-site projects for Hamid Marine at the Dyna-Mac shipyard. He maintained a strong relationship with the primary client and managed the daily deployment of the foreign workforce. He redeployed workers who were not active on Dyna-Mac projects to other Mectrade Group companies under established inter-company labor supply arrangements. Following the revocation of permits by the Ministry of Manpower, he coordinated salary payments and repatriation efforts through Dyna-Mac.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: A claim for breach of duty must be grounded in a recognised cause of action and supported by clear pleadings. In the absence of a formal directorship or a proven role as a shadow director, fiduciary standards are not engaged. However, operational management, including the supervision of projects and the redeployment of underutilised foreign workers under established group labor supply arrangements, is commercially rational and does not constitute a diversion of corporate value. Coordinating payments and worker repatriation in response to the revocation of permits by regulatory authorities is a necessary operational reaction to external constraints rather than an act causing corporate loss. Without proof of an actionable breach or a causal link to financial damage, liability is not established.
